name: Djibouti (Republic of Djibouti / République de Djibouti / جمهورية جيبوتي)
summary: Djibouti is a Horn of Africa mixed-legal-system republic combining French civil-law substantive heritage (via colonial inheritance) with Shafi'i Islamic-law personal-status jurisdiction (Djibouti being predominantly Muslim) operating through Sharia/Cadi Courts. Family-law framework operates under the Family Code 2002 (Code de la Famille, Law 152/AN/02/4ème L) drawing on Shafi'i Islamic-law jurisprudence with civil-law codification. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 76-96. The Supreme Court (Cour Suprême) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Council (Conseil Constitutionnel) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Cadi Court (Tribunal du Cadi) for Muslim personal-status matters and the Court of First Instance for civil-law matters. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Djibouti is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. Djibouti is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Family Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code drawing on Shafi'i Islamic-law jurisprudence with civil-law codification. Arts. 76-96 govern parental authority and child custody.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court (Cour Suprême)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Council (Conseil Constitutionnel)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Council with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, Djibouti
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Djiboutian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Djibouti independence + Gouled Aptidon presidency
key_developments.description: Djibouti achieved independence 27 June 1977 from France — among the latest African independence dates. Hassan Gouled Aptidon presidency 1977-1999 (22 years) under single-party framework until 1992 democratic transition.

key_developments.title: Djibouti ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Djibouti ratified the UNCRC on 6 December 1990 — among the early African ratifications globally. Framing the family-law-reform trajectory toward best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within mixed-legal-system framework.

key_developments.title: Constitution 1992 + multi-party transition + Civil War context
key_developments.description: Constitution of Djibouti adopted 4 September 1992 by referendum (substantially amended 2010) — establishing semi-presidential republic framework. Djiboutian Civil War 1991-1994 between government and Afar rebel forces (FRUD) substantially disrupted political-institutional framework.

key_developments.title: Ismaïl Omar Guelleh presidency succession + dynastic-presidency-continuation
key_developments.description: Ismaïl Omar Guelleh (nephew of Gouled Aptidon) assumed presidency 8 May 1999 — substantively distinctive African dynastic-presidency-continuation pattern. Subsequent re-elections 2005, 2011, 2016, 2021 — presidency continuing as of 2026.

key_developments.title: Arab League membership + bilingual-French-Arabic framework
key_developments.description: Djibouti joined Arab League 4 September 1977 (founding membership trajectory). Bilingual French-Arabic official-language framework substantively reflects colonial-French + Arab-League-Arab-cultural heritage. Substantive Arabic-Sharia-law-civil-codification reflecting Arab League framework.

key_developments.title: Family Code 2002 (Law 152/AN/02/4ème L)
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted 31 January 2002 (Law 152/AN/02/4ème L) drawing on Shafi'i Islamic-law jurisprudence with civil-law codification. Arts. 76-96 govern parental authority and child custody. Substantively distinctive Shafi'i-codification within Horn of Africa cluster.

key_developments.title: Doraleh Container Terminal + Belt and Road context + Chinese military base 2017
key_developments.description: Doraleh Container Terminal opened 2009 — substantively significant Belt and Road Initiative context affecting Djibouti's geopolitical positioning. Chinese military base opened 2017 — Djibouti hosts US, French, Italian, Japanese, Chinese military bases (most foreign-military-presence-per-capita globally). Substantive geopolitical position affecting family-law-cross-border-jurisdiction practice.

key_developments.title: Constitutional reform 2010 + term-limit modifications
key_developments.description: Constitutional reform 2010 substantively modified term-limit provisions enabling Guelleh's continued candidacy. Substantive contested constitutional reform within Horn of Africa framework.

key_developments.title: Anti-Domestic Violence framework consolidation + child-protection
key_developments.description: Anti-Domestic Violence framework consolidated through 2010s with various legislative interventions. Children's rights framework expanded within Shafi'i Islamic-law-civil-codification + Cadi-Court-civil-law-hybrid jurisdiction.

key_developments.title: IGAD framework + AU framework + Commission Nationale des Droits de l'Homme institutional position
key_developments.description: Djibouti IGAD (Intergovernmental Authority on Development) framework substantively significant Horn of Africa institutional context. African Union framework engagement substantively significant for continental-level child-rights framework. Commission Nationale des Droits de l'Homme institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Family Code framework. Substantively significant Horn of Africa Islamic-law institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Cour Suprême + Cadi Courts + Conseil Constitutionnel — interest-of-the-child substantive register
key_developments.description: Cour Suprême and Cadi Courts (for Muslim personal-status matters) continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code arts. 76-96 + Shafi'i-Islamic-law-civil-codification framework in custody disputes without adopting the 'parental alienation' label as a doctrinal term. Substantive analysis within mixed-legal-system + Cadi Court framework operating within Guelleh-dynastic-presidency political context.

structural_findings: Djibouti operates a mixed-legal-system framework — French civil-law substantive + Shafi'i Islamic-law personal-status via Cadi Courts. Within the Horn of Africa mixed-legal-system cluster.
Bilingual official-language framework (French + Arabic) reflects colonial-inheritance and Arab League membership heritage.
Non-Hague Convention status places Djibouti in the non-Hague Horn of Africa cluster.
editorial_notes: Djibouti jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-08 from 1 to 10 key_developments with full Gouled-Aptidon-to-Guelleh trajectory: 1977-Djibouti-independence-+-Gouled-Aptidon-presidency + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1992-Constitution-+-multi-party-+-Civil-War + 1999-Ismaïl-Omar-Guelleh-presidency-succession-+-dynastic-continuation + 2001-Arab-League-membership-context + 2002-Family-Code-Law-152-AN-02-4ème-L + 2007-Doraleh-Belt-and-Road-+-Chinese-military-base-2017 + 2010-Constitutional-reform-+-term-limit-modifications + 2017-Anti-Domestic-Violence-+-child-protection + 2024-Cour-Suprême-+-Cadi-Courts-+-Conseil-Constitutionnel-interest-of-the-child.
Mixed-legal-system Horn of Africa (French civil-law + Shafi'i Islamic-law via Cadi Courts + Family Code 2002 + bilingual French-Arabic + non-Hague Convention).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Family Code arts. 76-96 + Shafi'i-Islamic-law-civil-codification + Cadi-Court-civil-law-hybrid framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Horn-of-Africa + mixed-legal-system + Shafi'i-Islamic-law (with Comoros, Mayotte) + Cadi-Courts-Muslim-personal-status (with Comoros, Kenyan Kadhi, Mayotte) + Arab-League-membership-bilingual-French-Arabic + Gouled-Aptidon-Guelleh-dynastic-presidency-49-years + multi-foreign-military-base-host-globally-distinctive-Belt-and-Road-context + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
